comments:
Collision — Collision detection and contact resolution primitives.

## Design Philosophy

Collision detection is fundamental to physics simulation and UI interaction.
This module provides bounded, composable collision primitives that work at
billion-agent scale — deterministic detection with no edge cases.

## Collision Categories

**Bounding Volumes**: AABB, Sphere, Capsule, OBB
**Detection Results**: Contact points, normals, penetration depth
**Response Types**: Bounce, slide, stick, separate

## Bounded Design

All collision results are bounded:
- Penetration depth is clamped to prevent numerical explosion
- Contact normals are always unit vectors
- Contact counts are finite and bounded

## Dependencies

- Prelude (Eq, Ord, Show)
- Data.Number (sqrt, abs)
- Schema.Physics.Force (Force2D for collision response)

## Dependents

- Motion.Physics (physics engine)
- Component.Draggable (drag boundaries)
- Component.Sortable (item collision)

declarations.comments:
2D point for collision geometry.

Local definition to avoid circular dependencies with Geometry.Point.
declarations.title: Point2D

declarations.comments: Create a 2D point
declarations.title: point2D

declarations.comments: Origin point (0, 0)
declarations.title: origin2D

declarations.comments: Distance between two points
declarations.title: distance

declarations.comments: Squared distance (faster, avoids sqrt)
declarations.title: distanceSquared

declarations.comments: Midpoint between two points
declarations.title: midpoint

declarations.comments: Add a vector (offset) to a point
declarations.title: offsetPoint

declarations.comments:
Axis-Aligned Bounding Box.

The simplest and fastest collision primitive. Defined by min/max corners.
"Axis-aligned" means edges are parallel to coordinate axes — no rotation.
declarations.title: AABB

declarations.comments:
Create an AABB from min/max coordinates.
Automatically normalizes so min <= max.
declarations.title: aabb

declarations.comments: Create AABB from two corner points
declarations.title: aabbFromPoints

declarations.comments: Get AABB center point
declarations.title: aabbCenter

declarations.comments: Get AABB size (width, height)
declarations.title: aabbSize

declarations.comments: Get AABB half-size (useful for collision math)
declarations.title: aabbHalfSize

declarations.comments: Check if point is inside AABB
declarations.title: aabbContains

declarations.comments: Expand AABB by a margin on all sides
declarations.title: aabbExpand

declarations.comments: Merge two AABBs into one that contains both
declarations.title: aabbMerge

declarations.comments:
Bounding Circle (Bounding Sphere in 2D).

Rotationally invariant — rotation doesn't change the bounds.
Simpler math than AABB for moving objects.
declarations.title: BoundingCircle

declarations.comments: Create a bounding circle
declarations.title: boundingCircle

declarations.comments: Check if point is inside circle
declarations.title: circleContains

declarations.comments: Expand circle radius
declarations.title: circleExpand

declarations.comments: Merge two circles into one containing both
declarations.title: circleMerge

declarations.comments:
Bounding Capsule (Stadium shape).

Two circles connected by a rectangle. Good for elongated moving objects
(characters, bullets, etc.). Also called "swept sphere" or "stadium".
declarations.title: BoundingCapsule

declarations.comments: Create a bounding capsule
declarations.title: boundingCapsule

declarations.comments: Check if point is inside capsule
declarations.title: capsuleContains

declarations.comments:
Oriented Bounding Box.

Like AABB but can be rotated. More expensive to test but tighter fit
for rotated objects.
declarations.title: OBB

declarations.comments: Create an oriented bounding box
declarations.title: obb

declarations.comments: Get the four corners of the OBB
declarations.title: obbCorners

declarations.comments: Check if point is inside OBB
declarations.title: obbContains

declarations.comments:
Contact information from collision detection.

Contains everything needed to resolve a collision:
- Where the collision happened (contact point)
- Which direction to push apart (normal)
- How much they overlap (penetration depth)
declarations.title: Contact

declarations.comments: Create a contact
declarations.title: contact

declarations.comments: No contact result
declarations.title: noContact

declarations.comments: Check if there is a contact
declarations.title: hasContact

declarations.comments: Get contact point (origin if no contact)
declarations.title: contactPoint

declarations.comments: Get contact normal (zero if no contact)
declarations.title: contactNormal

declarations.comments: Get penetration depth (0 if no contact)
declarations.title: contactDepth

declarations.comments: Flip contact (swap which object is "first")
declarations.title: flipContact

declarations.comments: AABB vs AABB collision test
declarations.title: aabbVsAABB

declarations.comments: AABB vs Point collision test
declarations.title: aabbVsPoint

declarations.comments: AABB vs Circle collision test
declarations.title: aabbVsCircle

declarations.comments: Circle vs Circle collision test
declarations.title: circleVsCircle

declarations.comments: Circle vs Point collision test
declarations.title: circleVsPoint

declarations.comments: Circle vs AABB (flipped aabbVsCircle)
declarations.title: circleVsAABB

declarations.comments: Capsule vs Point collision test
declarations.title: capsuleVsPoint

declarations.comments: Capsule vs Capsule collision test
declarations.title: capsuleVsCapsule

declarations.comments: Collision response type.
declarations.title: CollisionResponse

declarations.comments: No collision response
declarations.title: responseNone

declarations.comments: Bounce response with restitution coefficient
declarations.title: responseBounce

declarations.comments: Slide response with friction coefficient
declarations.title: responseSlide

declarations.comments: Stick response (no sliding)
declarations.title: responseStick

declarations.comments:
Calculate position offset to resolve overlap.

Returns (dx, dy) to add to position to separate objects.
declarations.title: resolveOverlap

declarations.comments:
Calculate bounce velocity.

Reflects velocity around contact normal with restitution.
declarations.title: calculateBounce

declarations.comments:
Calculate slide velocity.

Projects velocity onto surface tangent with friction.
declarations.title: calculateSlide

declarations.comments:
Collision layer for filtering.

Objects on the same layer can collide. Layers are bit flags.
declarations.title: CollisionLayer

declarations.comments: Collision mask (which layers to collide with).
declarations.title: CollisionMask

declarations.comments: Check if a layer matches a mask
declarations.title: layerCollides

declarations.comments: Mask that collides with all layers
declarations.title: allLayers

declarations.comments: Mask that collides with no layers
declarations.title: noLayers

declarations.comments: Current collision state between two objects.
declarations.title: CollisionState

declarations.comments: Check if currently colliding
declarations.title: isColliding

declarations.comments: Check if separating
declarations.title: isSeparating

declarations.comments: Check if resting contact
declarations.title: isResting
